title: Kashmir at the Crossroads: Inside a 21st-Century Conflict
description: Kashmir at the Crossroads: Inside a 21st-Century Conflict is a book by Sumantra Bose. In August 2025, a notification was issued by Chandraker Bharti, Principal Secretary of the Jammu and Kashmir Home Department, saying: “…it has come to the notice of the Government, that certain literature propagates false narrative and secessionism in Jammu and Kashmir… This literature would deeply impact the psyche of youth by promoting (a) culture of grievance, victimhood and terrorist heroism.”
descriptionParagraphs: Kashmir at the Crossroads: Inside a 21st-Century Conflict is a book by Sumantra Bose. In August 2025, a notification was issued by Chandraker Bharti, Principal Secretary of the Jammu and Kashmir Home Department, saying: “…it has come to the notice of the Government, that certain literature propagates false narrative and secessionism in Jammu and Kashmir… This literature would deeply impact the psyche of youth by promoting (a) culture of grievance, victimhood and terrorist heroism.”

What makes it interesting is the way a book becomes legible to officials as a political instrument rather than a neutral cultural object. As a book, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in India. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: In August 2025, a notification was issued by Chandraker Bharti, Principal Secretary of the Jammu and Kashmir Home Department, saying: “…it has come to the notice of the Government, that certain literature propagates. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: Kashmir at the Crossroads: Inside a 21st-Century Conflict is a book by Sumantra Bose. In August 2025, a notification was issued by Chandraker Bharti, Principal Secretary of the Jammu and Kashmir Home Department, saying: “…it has come to the notice of the Government, that certain literature propagates false narrative and secessionism in Jammu and Kashmir… This literature would deeply impact the psyche of youth by promoting (a) culture of grievance, victimhood and terrorist heroism.”
banningParagraphs: Kashmir at the Crossroads: Inside a 21st-Century Conflict entered censorship debates as a book associated with politics, public argument, and state power. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political control and political dissent.

The earliest event currently captured here is 2025 in India, where Government of Jammu and Kashmir banned publication, sale, or possession. In August 2025, a notification was issued by Chandraker Bharti, Principal Secretary of the Jammu and Kashmir Home Department, saying: “…it has come to the notice of the Government, that certain literature propagates. counterReadings.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
counterReadings.author: Hannah Arendt
counterReadings.note: A foundational analysis of state terror, propaganda, and ideological conformity.

counterReadings.title: On Tyranny
counterReadings.author: Timothy Snyder
counterReadings.note: A short modern guide to resisting authoritarian politics and controlled public discourse.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
